name: laboratory_primate_enrichment_cut_budget
premise: A biomedical research institute faces a sudden grant shortfall and must cut costs within two weeks. Facilities director Dr. Owen Lin proposes reducing primate enrichment activities—fewer social hours, fewer toys, fewer outdoor rotations—to keep a critical gene therapy study running. Animal welfare officer Dr. Sara Ahmed warns that cutting enrichment will increase stress behaviors, compromise data validity, and violate the institute's ethical commitments. If the study pauses, staff layoffs are likely and a rare-disease advocacy group will lose momentum after years of fundraising. Regulators can audit at any time. Owen argues that the cuts are temporary and within minimum standards; Sara argues minimum standards are not the institute's promise to the public.

agents.name: Dr. Owen Lin
agents.identity: I am the facilities director responsible for keeping critical research operational when budget shocks force trade-offs between animal welfare and program survival.
agents.goal: I want to preserve a high-impact study without normalizing welfare cuts that harm animals and undermine scientific credibility.

agents.name: Dr. Sara Ahmed
agents.identity: I am the animal welfare officer accountable for ensuring that care practices meet ethical commitments and produce reliable science.
agents.goal: I want the institute to treat enrichment as essential, not optional, because stress-related harm is both unethical and scientifically corrosive.

choices: I will protect enrichment standards and pause the study while seeking emergency bridge funding, accepting schedule and staffing consequences to uphold ethics and data integrity.
I will implement a time-limited enrichment reduction with enhanced monitoring and a public justification, accepting ethical discomfort to keep the study and jobs afloat.
forced_choice.call_to_action: A hard budget deadline is approaching. Dr. Owen Lin must choose between pausing the study to protect enrichment standards or cutting enrichment temporarily to keep the work running. What does Dr. Owen Lin do?
